food_subgroup: Fried chicken sharing platter with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component party pack. FOODON_00002703 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the dominant component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken meat, fried) is used as the primary anchor. The pack also contains french fries and ranch dip, which are not captured by this single code; composite meal coding is not available in FoodEx2 at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a commercial fried chicken party pack product. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 party pack (7–8 persons): 20 boneless wings, 20 classic wings, 10 tenders, 6 regular fries, 6 ranch dips
serving.natural_language_portion: One party pack serves 7–8 people and contains approximately 3,200 g total, comprising 20 boneless wings, 20 classic wings, 10 chicken tenders, 6 portions of regular fries, and 6 ranch dipping sauces; a realistic individual portion is approximately 400 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, fried, coated; Chicken breast strips, fried; French fries, fast food; Ranch dressing
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry covers this composite party pack. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of the four component categories using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries: (1) Chicken wing, fried, coated (FDC ID 171477); (2) Chicken breast strips, fried (FDC ID 173964); (3) French fries, fast food (FDC ID 170720); (4) Ranch dressing (FDC ID 172237). Estimated component weight proportions: boneless wings ~28%, classic wings ~28%, tenders ~22%, fries ~17%, ranch dip ~5%. Values represent a blended per-100 g profile of the full pack.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database: French fries / fried potato (GI ~63–70); breaded fried chicken (GI ~60–65)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Wingstop party pack. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant glycemic contributors: french fries (GI ~63–70, University of Sydney GI Database) and breaded fried chicken coating (GI ~60–65, analogous to battered/breaded chicken per Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr). Ranch dip and chicken protein components suppress overall GI slightly. Glycemic load per individual serving (~400 g, ~65.6 g carbohydrate) = (65 × 65.6) / 100 ≈ 43 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2,080 mg per individual 400 g portion)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product, as a single-person serving provides approximately 2,080 mg sodium — approaching or exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — and the seasoned coatings and ranch dip contribute additional hidden sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (65), high carbohydrate load from breaded coating and french fries, and high total energy density
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this product: the breaded coating and french fries deliver a substantial glycemic load (~43 g GL per individual portion), and the high fat content may impair postprandial insulin sensitivity; portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This product is high in saturated fat (~3.6 g/100 g), contains detectable trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and delivers approximately 72 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit portion size to reduce atherogenic lipid and sodium exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Wingstop Party Pack (7–8 pax) is a large-format sharing meal sold at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore via field survey. It contains 20 boneless wings (in two flavours), 20 classic bone-in wings (in two flavours), 10 chicken tenders (in two flavours), 6 portions of regular french fries, and 6 ranch dipping sauces. All chicken pieces are deep-fried with a seasoned flour coating and finished in Wingstop's signature sauces, which range from mild (e.g., Lemon Pepper, Garlic Parmesan) to spicy (e.g., Original Hot, Atomic). The pack is marketed as Halal-certified in Singapore. Nutritionally, the pack is energy-dense (~265 kcal/100 g), high in protein (~16.8 g/100 g) from the chicken, and moderately high in fat (~15.2 g/100 g) due to deep-frying. Sodium is elevated (~520 mg/100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The breaded coating and french fries contribute a medium glycemic index (~65) and a high glycemic load per individual serving (~43 g GL), which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The pack is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. It is best consumed as an occasional shared meal rather than a regular dietary staple. Typical allergens include wheat (breading), milk (ranch dip), and possible egg and soy in the seasoning.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Wingstop Party Pack?
How much protein in Wingstop wings?
Is Wingstop party pack suitable for diabetics?
How many people does Wingstop party pack serve?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry covers this exact composite commercial party pack. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average blending of four USDA FoodData Central component entries (fried chicken wing, fried chicken breast strips, fast-food french fries, ranch dressing). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for french fries and breaded fried chicken. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per-100 g estimates derived from a weighted-average blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy/Foundation Foods entries for the four main component categories. Exact Wingstop proprietary formulations are not publicly disclosed. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component USDA entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component USDA entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in all component USDA entries used for this composite estimate. 5. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) based on analogous foods from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this product exists in the approved literature. 6. The pack is listed as Halal on field survey data; this is preserved as a dietary flag. 7. Trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA data for deep-fried breaded chicken; actual value may vary by frying oil used by Wingstop Singapore. 8. Serving size of 3,200 g total and 400 g per individual portion are estimates based on typical Wingstop component weights; exact weights are not publicly disclosed. 9. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g values are estimated from USDA component data and should be treated as approximate.
